service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

requirements: Only for first four people who arrive each day

Free clothing and toiletries as available (be aware that most of the clothing to be given out is for men).

GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name and age.

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

On-site specialties include cardiology (heart), dermatology (skin), vision, endocrinology (hormones and glands), and pulmonary problems (lung).  Spanish translators available during all clinic hours.

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Allegheny Link Line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.   For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   For the next few months, the Link will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Light of Life Emergency Shelter (for Men)
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name and age.
narrative: When the Link Line is closed, single men 25 and over can seek emergency overnight shelter directly at the Light of Life Rescue Mission at 10 East North Ave on the North Side.

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.
narrative: When the Link Line is closed, women 25 and over may seek emergency overnight shelter at Bethlehem Haven, 1410 Fifth Ave in Uptown.  (This is a temporary location while their building on Watson St is under construction.)

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

requirements: Only for first four people who arrive each day
narrative: You may wash clothes free of charge.

service_name: Pleasant Valley Men's Shelter - Daytime Drop-In
narrative: Place to hang out on the North Side on weekdays with a TV room and library. Free lunch served M/W/F. Lockers available to store belongings during drop-in hours. Laundry can be done on a first-come, first-served basis for up to four people. Showers available for men only. Free clothing and toiletries as available (be aware that most of the clothing to be given out is for men).
Showers for men only, toiletries provided by shelter.

service_name: Pleasant Valley Men's Shelter - Free Supplies
narrative: Free clothing and toiletries can be given out as available (be aware that most of the clothing is for men).

recommended_for: anyone experiencing a mental health crisis
requirements: none; walk-ins welcome; mobile trained counselors can come to you
narrative: RE:SOLVE CRISIS NETWORK has mental health professionals 24/7 to help.  Same day confidential assessment. Temporary stay-overs possible based on diagnosis.

requirements: Photo ID not required and self-certify income (up to 150% of poverty line, which they do not verify) with signature. It is recommended to bring bags to carry food in, and some form of entertainment such as a book, as there can be long waits.
narrative: Healthy food is distributed at this pantry; please do not use more than once per month.  Student nurses provide free health screening and referrals.  Cooking demos and nutrition classes offered. Also has free pet food through the Chow Wagon program.

service_name: Tues/Thurs/Sun Meal - Hot Metal Bridge Faith Community
narrative: \The Table\" is a free community meal hosted every Tuesday and Thursday as well as a hot lunch on Sundays.  Meals are prepared by volunteer groups from all over the city.  During meals, 412  Food Rescue distributes fruit, veggies and other food to take away.  The Care Closet stocks clothing, camping supplies and toiletries for those on the street."

service_name: Hot Metal Bridge Faith Community Care Closet
narrative: Clothing (mostly adult sizes), shoes, blankets, and toiletries (including tampons and pads) available for anyone in need. Sometimes sleeping bags, tents, and backpacks available as well.

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Routine dental services available (cleanings, exams and x-rays, fillings and extractions). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Birmingham Free Clinic - Mon, Wed, Sat
requirements: none, for patients without health insurance; first-come, first-served; no appointments
narrative: Free walk-in clinic for both primary and acute care.  On-site pharmacy for medications (with no narcotics).  Services include physicals, chronic disease management, flu vaccines and health education.  On-site specialties include cardiology (heart), dermatology (skin), vision, endocrinology (hormones and glands), and pulmonary problems (lung).  Spanish translators available during all clinic hours.
schedule: Sign-In Hours:; Mon:  at 5pm; Wed: at 12pm; Sat: at 9am; Appointments available Tues 1pm - 4pm, and Wed 8:30am - 11:30am

Adult Services: Provides a range of adult immunizations, including influenza and pneumococcal vaccines at the walk-in Clinic in downtown Pittsburgh. ; ; Childhood Immunizations: Federally funded and administered free of charge to eligible children. Additionally, Compliance with Allegheny County and Pennsylvania School Immunization Laws is assured through regular audits of school health records. Find CDC recommended Immunization Schedules external link.; ; WIC Certification: Screening for hemoglobin, weight and height are included for certification and re-certification for the Women, Infants and Children Program.; ; Travel Immunizations: Arranged for persons of all ages, as determined after appropriate travel advice meeting.; ; Screening Tests: Blood testing, to determine antibody levels for diseases such as hepatitis B, rubella and measles, is offered.

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps. service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children

organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.

Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Allegheny Link Line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.   For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   For the next few months, the Link will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Light of Life Emergency Shelter (for Men)
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name and age.
narrative: When the Link Line is closed, single men 25 and over can seek emergency overnight shelter directly at the Light of Life Rescue Mission at 10 East North Ave on the North Side.

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.
narrative: When the Link Line is closed, women 25 and over may seek emergency overnight shelter at Bethlehem Haven, 1410 Fifth Ave in Uptown.  (This is a temporary location while their building on Watson St is under construction.)

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

requirements: Only for first four people who arrive each day
narrative: You may wash clothes free of charge.

service_name: Pleasant Valley Men's Shelter - Daytime Drop-In

Laundry can be done on a first-come, first-served basis for up to four people. Showers available for men only. Free clothing and toiletries as available (be aware that most of the clothing to be given out is for men).
Showers for men only, toiletries provided by shelter.

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Routine dental services available (cleanings, exams and x-rays, fillings and extractions). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Birmingham Free Clinic - Mon, Wed, Sat
requirements: none, for patients without health insurance; first-come, first-served; no appointments
narrative: Free walk-in clinic for both primary and acute care.  On-site pharmacy for medications (with no narcotics).  Services include physicals, chronic disease management, flu vaccines and health education.  On-site specialties include cardiology (heart), dermatology (skin), vision, endocrinology (hormones and glands), and pulmonary problems (lung).  Spanish translators available during all clinic hours.

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies. For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

narrative: When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.   For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   For the next few months, the Link will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Light of Life Emergency Shelter (for Men)
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name and age.
narrative: When the Link Line is closed, single men 25 and over can seek emergency overnight shelter directly at the Light of Life Rescue Mission at 10 East North Ave on the North Side.

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.
narrative: When the Link Line is closed, women 25 and over may seek emergency overnight shelter at Bethlehem Haven, 1410 Fifth Ave in Uptown.  (This is a temporary location while their building on Watson St is under construction.)

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

requirements: Only for first four people who arrive each day
narrative: You may wash clothes free of charge.

service_name: Pleasant Valley Men's Shelter - Daytime Drop-In
narrative: Place to hang out on the North Side on weekdays with a TV room and library. Free lunch served M/W/F. Lockers available to store belongings during drop-in hours. Laundry can be done on a first-come, first-served basis for up to four people. Showers available for men only. Free clothing and toiletries as available (be aware that most of the clothing to be given out is for men).
Showers for men only, toiletries provided by shelter.

service_name: Pleasant Valley Men's Shelter - Free Supplies
narrative: Free clothing and toiletries can be given out as available (be aware that most of the clothing is for men).

recommended_for: anyone experiencing a mental health crisis
requirements: none; walk-ins welcome; mobile trained counselors can come to you
narrative: RE:SOLVE CRISIS NETWORK has mental health professionals 24/7 to help.  Same day confidential assessment. Temporary stay-overs possible based on diagnosis.

requirements: Photo ID not required and self-certify income (up to 150% of poverty line, which they do not verify) with signature. It is recommended to bring bags to carry food in, and some form of entertainment such as a book, as there can be long waits.
narrative: Healthy food is distributed at this pantry; please do not use more than once per month.  Student nurses provide free health screening and referrals.  Cooking demos and nutrition classes offered. Also has free pet food through the Chow Wagon program.

requirements: must attend worship service before meal
narrative: Free Sunday meal following worship service.

service_name: Tues/Thurs/Sun Meal - Hot Metal Bridge Faith Community
narrative: \The Table\" is a free community meal hosted every Tuesday and Thursday as well as a hot lunch on Sundays.  Meals are prepared by volunteer groups from all over the city.  During meals, 412  Food Rescue distributes fruit, veggies and other food to take away.  The Care Closet stocks clothing, camping supplies and toiletries for those on the street."

service_name: Hot Metal Bridge Faith Community Care Closet
narrative: Clothing (mostly adult sizes), shoes, blankets, and toiletries (including tampons and pads) available for anyone in need. Sometimes sleeping bags, tents, and backpacks available as well.

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Routine dental services available (cleanings, exams and x-rays, fillings and extractions). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Birmingham Free Clinic - Mon, Wed, Sat
requirements: none, for patients without health insurance; first-come, first-served; no appointments
narrative: Free walk-in clinic for both primary and acute care.  On-site pharmacy for medications (with no narcotics).  Services include physicals, chronic disease management, flu vaccines and health education.  On-site specialties include cardiology (heart), dermatology (skin), vision, endocrinology (hormones and glands), and pulmonary problems (lung).  Spanish translators available during all clinic hours.
schedule: Sign-In Hours:; Mon:  at 5pm; Wed: at 12pm; Sat: at 9am; Appointments available Tues 1pm - 4pm, and Wed 8:30am - 11:30am

requirements: none, for patients without health insurance; first-come, first-served; no appointments
narrative: Free pediatric (Dr. for children) clinic every second Saturday of the month.  Spanish speakers available (hablantes de español disponibles).
schedule: Second Saturday of the Month:; 9am sign-in

recommended_for: all with STD/HIV/AIDS/Hep C concerns and Transgender patients
requirements: free STD/HIV testing; fees on sliding scale (no one turned away); appointments recommended
narrative: Practice with specialty in HIV positive and Transgender patients.  Leading the field in HIV prevention with PrEP.  Will help with signing up for insurance and other assistance.
schedule: Mon:  11am - 8pm; Tues - Thurs:  8:30am - 5pm; Fri:  10am - 4pm (designated walk-in day)

service_name: Central Outreach Resource & Referral Center (testing & support)
recommended_for: those with STD/HepC/HIV concerns or with drug and alcohol issues
narrative: Center offers free STD/HepC/HIV testing and provides for drug and alcohol treatment referrals.  Also provides supportive and educational services.

Staffed by nurses and public health assistants, the clinic provides free and confidential examinations and treatment.  Tests for syphilis, gonorrhea, chlamydia and HIV routinely done; other tests as indicated.  Lab inside building, so test results often same-day.
schedule: Mon, Tues, Thurs, Fri:  8:30am - 3:30pm; Wed:  12:30 - 7:30pm; Doors open 30 minutes ahead of time for registration only.

Adult Services: Provides a range of adult immunizations, including influenza and pneumococcal vaccines at the walk-in Clinic in downtown Pittsburgh. ; ; Childhood Immunizations: Federally funded and administered free of charge to eligible children. Additionally, Compliance with Allegheny County and Pennsylvania School Immunization Laws is assured through regular audits of school health records. Find CDC recommended Immunization Schedules external link.; ; WIC Certification: Screening for hemoglobin, weight and height are included for certification and re-certification for the Women, Infants and Children Program.; ; Travel Immunizations: Arranged for persons of all ages, as determined after appropriate travel advice meeting.; ; Screening Tests: Blood testing, to determine antibody levels for diseases such as hepatitis B, rubella and measles, is offered.
Free preventative and basic restorative dental care for children 1-20 years of age.

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Allegheny Link Line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.   For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   For the next few months, the Link will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.
narrative: When the Link Line is closed, women 25 and over may seek emergency overnight shelter at Bethlehem Haven, 1410 Fifth Ave in Uptown.  (This is a temporary location while their building on Watson St is under construction.)

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Routine dental services available (cleanings, exams and x-rays, fillings and extractions). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Birmingham Free Clinic - Mon, Wed, Sat
requirements: none, for patients without health insurance; first-come, first-served; no appointments
narrative: Free walk-in clinic for both primary and acute care.  On-site pharmacy for medications (with no narcotics). service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Allegheny Link Line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.   For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   For the next few months, the Link will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children

requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.

Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Allegheny Link Line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.   For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   For the next few months, the Link will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name and age.
narrative: When the Link Line is closed, single men 25 and over can seek emergency overnight shelter directly at the Light of Life Rescue Mission at 10 East North Ave on the North Side.

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.
narrative: When the Link Line is closed, women 25 and over may seek emergency overnight shelter at Bethlehem Haven, 1410 Fifth Ave in Uptown.  (This is a temporary location while their building on Watson St is under construction.)

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Routine dental services available (cleanings, exams and x-rays, fillings and extractions). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Birmingham Free Clinic - Mon, Wed, Sat
requirements: none, for patients without health insurance; first-come, first-served; no appointments
narrative: Free walk-in clinic for both primary and acute care.  On-site pharmacy for medications (with no narcotics).  Services include physicals, chronic disease management, flu vaccines and health education.  On-site specialties include cardiology (heart), dermatology (skin), vision, endocrinology (hormones and glands), and pulmonary problems (lung).  Spanish translators available during all clinic hours.

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Allegheny Link Line for Emergency Overnight Shelter

requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.   For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   For the next few months, the Link will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults

(Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Routine dental services available (cleanings, exams and x-rays, fillings and extractions). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Birmingham Free Clinic - Mon, Wed, Sat
requirements: none, for patients without health insurance; first-come, first-served; no appointments
narrative: Free walk-in clinic for both primary and acute care.  On-site pharmacy for medications (with no narcotics). service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Allegheny Link Line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.   For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   For the next few months, the Link will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Allegheny Link Line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.   For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   For the next few months, the Link will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.

Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

service_name: Allegheny Link Line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.   For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   For the next few months, the Link will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services

organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked for social security number (not required), name and age.
narrative: When the Link Line is closed, women 25 and over may seek emergency overnight shelter at Bethlehem Haven, 1410 Fifth Ave in Uptown.  (This is a temporary location while their building on Watson St is under construction.)

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.

Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).   When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
A long Pittsburgh tradition
Free lunch for all in need

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).   When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

service_name: Pleasant Valley Men's Shelter - Daytime Drop-In
narrative: Place to hang out on the North Side on weekdays with a TV room and library. Free lunch served M/W/F. Lockers available to store belongings during drop-in hours. Laundry can be done on a first-come, first-served basis for up to four people. Showers available for men only. Free clothing and toiletries as available (be aware that most of the clothing to be given out is for men).

service_name: Tues/Thurs/Sun Meal - Hot Metal Bridge Faith Community
narrative: \The Table\" is a free community meal hosted every Tuesday and Thursday as well as a hot lunch on Sundays.  Meals are prepared by volunteer groups from all over the city.  During meals, 412  Food Rescue distributes fruit, veggies and other food to take away.  The Care Closet stocks clothing, camping supplies and toiletries for those on the street."

service_name: Hot Metal Bridge Faith Community Care Closet
narrative: Clothing (mostly adult sizes), shoes, blankets, and toiletries (including tampons and pads) available for anyone in need. Sometimes sleeping bags, tents, and backpacks available as well.

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Routine dental services available (cleanings, exams and x-rays, fillings and extractions). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Birmingham Free Clinic - Mon, Wed, Sat
requirements: none, for patients without health insurance; first-come, first-served; no appointments
narrative: Free walk-in clinic for both primary and acute care.  On-site pharmacy for medications (with no narcotics).  Services include physicals, chronic disease management, flu vaccines and health education.  On-site specialties include cardiology (heart), dermatology (skin), vision, endocrinology (hormones and glands), and pulmonary problems (lung).  Spanish translators available during all clinic hours.

Adult Services: Provides a range of adult immunizations, including influenza and pneumococcal vaccines at the walk-in Clinic in downtown Pittsburgh. ; ; Childhood Immunizations: Federally funded and administered free of charge to eligible children. Additionally, Compliance with Allegheny County and Pennsylvania School Immunization Laws is assured through regular audits of school health records. Find CDC recommended Immunization Schedules external link.; ; WIC Certification: Screening for hemoglobin, weight and height are included for certification and re-certification for the Women, Infants and Children Program.; ; Travel Immunizations: Arranged for persons of all ages, as determined after appropriate travel advice meeting.; ; Screening Tests: Blood testing, to determine antibody levels for diseases such as hepatitis B, rubella and measles, is offered.

requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).   When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

service_name: Pleasant Valley Men's Shelter - Daytime Drop-In
narrative: Place to hang out on the North Side on weekdays with a TV room and library. Free lunch served M/W/F. Lockers available to store belongings during drop-in hours. Laundry can be done on a first-come, first-served basis for up to four people. Showers available for men only. Free clothing and toiletries as available (be aware that most of the clothing to be given out is for men).

recommended_for: anyone experiencing a mental health crisis
requirements: none; walk-ins welcome; mobile trained counselors can come to you
narrative: RE:SOLVE CRISIS NETWORK has mental health professionals 24/7 to help.  Same day confidential assessment. Temporary stay-overs possible based on diagnosis.

requirements: Photo ID not required and self-certify income (up to 150% of poverty line, which they do not verify) with signature. It is recommended to bring bags to carry food in, and some form of entertainment such as a book, as there can be long waits.
narrative: Healthy food is distributed at this pantry; please do not use more than once per month.  Student nurses provide free health screening and referrals.  Cooking demos and nutrition classes offered. Also has free pet food through the Chow Wagon program.

requirements: must attend worship service before meal
narrative: Free Sunday meal following worship service.

service_name: Tues/Thurs/Sun Meal - Hot Metal Bridge Faith Community
narrative: \The Table\" is a free community meal hosted every Tuesday and Thursday as well as a hot lunch on Sundays.  Meals are prepared by volunteer groups from all over the city.  During meals, 412  Food Rescue distributes fruit, veggies and other food to take away.  The Care Closet stocks clothing, camping supplies and toiletries for those on the street."

service_name: Hot Metal Bridge Faith Community Care Closet
narrative: Clothing (mostly adult sizes), shoes, blankets, and toiletries (including tampons and pads) available for anyone in need. Sometimes sleeping bags, tents, and backpacks available as well.

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Routine dental services available (cleanings, exams and x-rays, fillings and extractions). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Birmingham Free Clinic - Mon, Wed, Sat
requirements: none, for patients without health insurance; first-come, first-served; no appointments
narrative: Free walk-in clinic for both primary and acute care.  On-site pharmacy for medications (with no narcotics).  Services include physicals, chronic disease management, flu vaccines and health education.  On-site specialties include cardiology (heart), dermatology (skin), vision, endocrinology (hormones and glands), and pulmonary problems (lung).  Spanish translators available during all clinic hours.
schedule: Sign-In Hours:; Mon:  at 5pm; Wed: at 1pm; Sat: at 9am; Appointments available Tues 12:30pm - 3:30pm, and Wed 8:30am - 11am (dial extension 281)

Adult Services: Provides a range of adult immunizations, including influenza and pneumococcal vaccines at the walk-in Clinic in downtown Pittsburgh. ; ; Childhood Immunizations: Federally funded and administered free of charge to eligible children. Additionally, Compliance with Allegheny County and Pennsylvania School Immunization Laws is assured through regular audits of school health records. Find CDC recommended Immunization Schedules external link.; ; WIC Certification: Screening for hemoglobin, weight and height are included for certification and re-certification for the Women, Infants and Children Program.; ; Travel Immunizations: Arranged for persons of all ages, as determined after appropriate travel advice meeting.; ; Screening Tests: Blood testing, to determine antibody levels for diseases such as hepatitis B, rubella and measles, is offered.
Free preventative and basic restorative dental care for children 1-20 years of age.

requirements: just call ahead for an appointment; children without access to insurance also taken care of

service_name: Allegheny Link line for Emergency Overnight Shelter
organization: Allegheny County Department of Human Services
recommended_for: for those who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness, those with a disability, or those over the age of 60 (with or without a disability)
requirements: For shelter, you will be asked to provide social security number (not required), name, gender and age.
narrative: For adult singles, the Link line will provide information on nearby shelters.  You will then need to reach out directly to a Shelter to get information about bed vacancies.   The Link line will not be reserving spaces or maintaining waiting lists for single shelters (including youth and veterans).  For families, the Link will continue to coordinate assignment to shelters.  The Link line can also offer referrals to a wide range of programs and services for those in distress (and help with completion of any needed paperwork).   When the Link line is closed, you will get a recorded message with options for those in particular need.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Homeless Young Adults
narrative: For youth ages 18 - 24 who are homeless or transient (or at risk of not being able to find a safe place to stay), Familylinks Shelter can provide emergency shelter, food and clothing for 60 days while they work with you to plan next steps.  (Even after 60 days, they will make sure you have a place to stay.)   Medical care and counseling can also be provided as needed.

service_name: Emergency Shelter for Women with Children
program_or_facility: Women's Center and Shelter of Greater Pittsburgh
organization: Women's Center and Shelter of Pittsburgh
requirements: to be admitted, you need to call to discuss your situation with an advocate.
narrative: For women with children (including boys 17 and under) who fear domestic violence and have no place else to go.  Shelter services include meeting basic needs like clothing, toiletries and meals.  Advocates provide safety planning, emotional support, crisis intervention and goal planning.  Average length of stay is 30 days, with some stays as long as 45 days.
Free lunch for all in need

service_name: Pleasant Valley Men's Shelter - Daytime Drop-In
narrative: Place to hang out on the North Side on weekdays with a TV room and library. Free lunch served M/W/F. Lockers available to store belongings during drop-in hours. Laundry can be done on a first-come, first-served basis for up to four people. Showers available for men only. Free clothing and toiletries as available (be aware that most of the clothing to be given out is for men).

recommended_for: anyone experiencing a mental health crisis
requirements: none; walk-ins welcome; mobile trained counselors can come to you
narrative: RE:SOLVE CRISIS NETWORK has mental health professionals 24/7 to help.  Same day confidential assessment. Temporary stay-overs possible based on diagnosis.

requirements: Photo ID not required and self-certify income (up to 150% of poverty line, which they do not verify) with signature. It is recommended to bring bags to carry food in, and some form of entertainment such as a book, as there can be long waits.

narrative: \The Table\" is a free community meal hosted every Tuesday and Thursday as well as a hot lunch on Sundays.  Meals are prepared by volunteer groups from all over the city.  During meals, 412  Food Rescue distributes fruit, veggies and other food to take away.  The Care Closet stocks clothing, camping supplies and toiletries for those on the street."

narrative: Clothing (mostly adult sizes), shoes, blankets, and toiletries (including tampons and pads) available for anyone in need. Sometimes sleeping bags, tents, and backpacks available as well.

service_name: Free Health Care Center - Catholic Charities
recommended_for: all 19 or older needing primary care (no emergency care)
requirements: not a walk-in Clinic, service by appointment to low-income eligible; does not treat children under 19
narrative: The Center offers both primary care physician level services as well as specialty care services (when specialists are available), including foot care (podiatry), mental health care, gynecology (women's health) and eye care (and glasses). Routine dental services available (cleanings, exams and x-rays, fillings and extractions). Specialty dental care also offered when specialists are available (oral surgery, root canals and selective restorative care). GO TO THE WELCOME CENTER to fill out eligibility form or go to freecarepgh.org website and click on \"Information about eligibility\" at bottom of first paragraph."

service_name: Birmingham Free Clinic - Mon, Wed, Sat
requirements: none, for patients without health insurance; first-come, first-served; no appointments
narrative: Free walk-in clinic for both primary and acute care.  On-site pharmacy for medications (with no narcotics).  Services include physicals, chronic disease management, flu vaccines and health education.  On-site specialties include cardiology (heart), dermatology (skin), vision, endocrinology (hormones and glands), and pulmonary problems (lung).  Spanish translators available during all clinic hours.
